title: Vertiv — Liquid Cooling Picks-and-Shovels for NVIDIA Rubin
catalyst: Vertiv's Q4 2025 organic orders grew ~252% YoY and backlog reached $15B (+109% YoY). Two 2026 acquisitions — Strategic Thermal Labs (April) and ThermoKey (June) — explicitly target high-density liquid cooling for NVIDIA's Rubin architecture, with Q2 2026 results due July 29, 2026.
thesis: Liquid cooling is the under-recognized capex line in the Rubin transition. As GPU TDPs cross 1,500W+, air-cooling stops working and CDU + rear-door + immersion penetration jumps from ~5% to majority share in new builds. Vertiv is the most consolidated Western pure-play — power, switchgear, AND thermal — with Rubin reference-design wins that the broader AI-power names (GEV, CEG, PWR, ETN already on the active sheet) don't capture. The angle is mechanism-specific: this is the thermal leg of the AI buildout, not the grid leg.

source.title: My Top 3 AI Infrastructure Stocks to Buy for May 2026

what_would_promote: Promote to On Our Radar if (a) Vertiv's July 29 Q2 print confirms backlog trajectory and raises FY guide, OR (b) NVIDIA names Vertiv as a Rubin reference-architecture partner in a published spec, OR (c) we can map a clean entry zone tied to the post-print reaction.
checks_remaining: Falsifiable dated catalyst exists (Q2 earnings 2026-07-29). Need a second primary source (SEC filing or NVIDIA partnership disclosure) — current source is Motley Fool which only counts as context, not toward the primary-source minimum. No trade construction yet.

title: Seagate — HAMR Nearline Storage Sold Out Through 2026
catalyst: Mozaic 4 (44TB HAMR) entered revenue shipments late March 2026 and is qualified by every major US hyperscaler. Production capacity for 30TB+ drives is reportedly sold out through calendar 2026 under build-to-order contracts that give 3-4 quarters of demand visibility.
thesis: The HBM/DRAM names already on the active sheet (MU, SNDK) capture the high-velocity AI memory leg, but the warm/cold storage leg — where ~80% of training data and model checkpoints live — is its own constrained market. Seagate is one of two viable suppliers, and HAMR is the only technology that scales to the $/TB hyperscalers need at the 40-50TB tier. The angle is storage tier (nearline, not HBM) and technology moat (HAMR-only at this capacity), so it's mechanism-distinct from the active memory opps.

source.title: Seagate (STX) Had a Blowout Q3. The AI Storage Bull Case Is Building

what_would_promote: Promote to On Our Radar after the next 10-Q confirms nearline mix shift and gross margin expansion from HAMR. Promote to High Conviction if Mozaic 4 ramp is accompanied by a multi-year supply agreement disclosure from a named hyperscaler.
checks_remaining: Need at least one direct primary source (Seagate 10-Q/8-K confirming Mozaic 4 revenue contribution and hyperscaler qualification) — current source is TipRanks summary. Also need to check that STX isn't already implicitly covered by SNDK/MU memory thesis on the active sheet (it is not — different storage tier).
